description: Benchmarks for notion-cli workspace cache (CLI with local SQLite+FTS5) vs Notion MCP server

evals.name: Workspace Search
evals.prompt: Search my Notion workspace for pages related to 'Support'. List the page names you find.
assertions: mentions Support Docs or Support AI
returns at least 2 results

evals.name: Page Content Lookup
evals.prompt: Find the page called 'Support AI Console' in my Notion workspace and tell me what it's about.
assertions: finds the Support AI Console page
describes its purpose or content

evals.name: Workspace Overview
evals.prompt: Give me a high-level overview of what's in my Notion workspace. What are the main sections or areas?
assertions: lists multiple workspace areas or sections
provides a structured overview

evals.name: Find Recent Pages
evals.prompt: Search my Notion workspace for pages related to 'plan' or 'planning'. What planning-related pages exist?
assertions: finds planning-related pages
lists page names

evals.name: Cross-Reference Search
evals.prompt: Search my Notion workspace for anything related to 'architecture' or 'API'. List what you find.
assertions: finds architecture or API related pages
lists at least 1 result

evals.name: Targeted Page Fetch
evals.prompt: Find the page about 'Idempotency Key Support' in my Notion workspace. What is the request ID mentioned?
assertions: finds the Idempotency Key Support page
mentions requestId or request ID

evals.name: Page Create and Cleanup
evals.prompt: Create a test page titled 'Benchmark Test Page' under Support Docs (page ID: 3262dbd5-708f-812f-9073-d2fda63adcde) with a paragraph saying 'This is a benchmark test.' Then verify it was created. Finally, archive the page you just created.
assertions: creates a page with the correct title
confirms creation was successful
archives or deletes the test page

evals.name: Database Create with Schema
evals.prompt: Create a test database titled 'Benchmark Tasks' under Support Docs (page ID: 3262dbd5-708f-812f-9073-d2fda63adcde) with columns: Name (title), Status (select with options: To Do, In Progress, Done), Priority (number). Then verify the schema was created correctly. Finally, archive the database.
assertions: creates a database with the correct schema
database has Name, Status, and Priority columns
archives or deletes the test database

evals.name: Workspace Hierarchy
evals.prompt: Show me the hierarchy/tree structure of my Notion workspace. What pages are nested under other pages?
assertions: shows workspace structure
shows parent-child relationships or mentions hierarchy

with_skill_instructions: You have notion-cli installed with a local workspace cache. Workflow: 1) Use `notion-cli workspace search <query>` to find pages by name (returns page IDs). 2) Use `notion-cli workspace get <query>` to search + auto-fetch top result content. 3) Use `notion-cli workspace pages` to list all pages. 4) Use `notion-cli workspace databases` to list databases. 5) Use `notion-cli workspace schema <db>` for database schemas. 6) Use `notion-cli workspace tree` for page hierarchy (supports --databases-only, --depth N). 7) For creating pages: `notion-cli pages create --parent-page-id <id> --title 'Title' --json`. 8) For creating databases with DDL: `notion-cli db create --parent-page-id <id> --title 'Name' --schema "Col1 TITLE, Col2 SELECT('A','B'), Col3 NUMBER"`. 9) For archiving: `notion-cli pages update <id> --archive --json`. The cache indexes pages, databases, and schemas.
without_skill_instructions: You have the Notion MCP server connected. Use the notion-search and notion-fetch MCP tools to find and read Notion pages.
